# Backend planning for document editor (2026-03-03 16:44Z)

_**User**_

This is a document editor project - look at the readme and code for more info. I have a mostly working frontend, but with minimal documentation. Now I want to plan and develop the backend. The main things I need are a way to store documents not locally on the browser with IndexedDB, which is what's happening now, and a secure way for users to log in. Let's start planning out how this backend is going to look - ask questions when clarification is needed. I would ideally like to not have to pay for this, and it needs to work locally as well as when deployed to AWS, which will happen later.

---

_**Agent (model gpt-5.3-codex, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Preparing to switch mode**</details></think>

---

This is a planning-focused request with architecture trade-offs (auth, storage, local + AWS), so I’m switching to Plan mode to map options clearly before implementation.

---

I’ll quickly inspect the repo structure and README plus where IndexedDB/auth are currently implemented, then I’ll ask only the critical architecture questions needed to finalize a backend plan.

IndexedDB utilities for GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface Document {\\\\\\\\n  id: string;\\\\\\\\n  title: string;\\\\\\\\n  content: string;\\\\\\\\n  language: 'en' | 'de' | 'ru';\\\\\\\\n  createdAt: number;\\\\\\\\n  updatedAt: number;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst DB_NAME = 'GlossaDocs';\\\\\\\\nconst STORE_NAME = 'documents';\\\\\\\\nconst DB_VERSION = 1;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nlet dbInstance: IDBDatabase | null = null;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Initialize the IndexedDB database\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function initDB(): Promise&lt;IDBDatabase&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  if (dbInstance) {\\\\\\\\n    return dbInstance;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onerror = () =&gt; reject(request.error);\\\\\\\\n    request.onsuccess = () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      dbInstance = request.result;\\\\\\\\n      resolve(request.result);\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onupgradeneeded = (event) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      const db = (event.target as IDBOpenDBRequest).result;\\\\\\\\n      if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(STORE_NAME)) {\\\\\\\\n        const objectStore = db.createObjectStore(STORE_NAME, { keyPath: 'id' });\\\\\\\\n        objectStore.createIndex('updatedAt', 'updatedAt', { unique: false });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Helper function to execute an IndexedDB transaction\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nasync function executeTransaction&lt;T&gt;(\\\\\\\\n  mode: IDBTransactionMode,\\\\\\\\n  operation: (store: IDBObjectStore) =&gt; IDBRequest&lt;T&gt;\\\\\\\\n): Promise&lt;T&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const db = await initDB();\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const transaction = db.transaction([STORE_NAME], mode);\\\\\\\\n    const objectStore = transaction.objectStore(STORE_NAME);\\\\\\\\n    const request = operation(objectStore);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onerror = () =&gt; reject(request.error);\\\\\\\\n    request.onsuccess = () =&gt; resolve(request.result);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Get all documents sorted by most recently updated\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function getAllDocuments(): Promise&lt;Document[]&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const docs = await executeTransaction&lt;Document[]&gt;('readonly', (store) =&gt; store.getAll());\\\\\\\\n  // Sort by most recently updated\\\\\\\\n  return docs.sort((a, b) =&gt; b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Get a single document by ID\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function getDocument(id: string): Promise&lt;Document | null&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const result = await executeTransaction&lt;Document | undefined&gt;('readonly', (store) =&gt;\\\\\\\\n    store.get(id)\\\\\\\\n  );\\\\\\\\n  return result || null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Save or update a document\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function saveDocument(doc: Document): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  await executeTransaction&lt;IDBValidKey&gt;('readwrite', (store) =&gt; store.put(doc));\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Delete a document by ID\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function deleteDocument(id: string): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  await executeTransaction&lt;undefined&gt;('readwrite', (store) =&gt; store.delete(id));\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Generate a unique ID for a document\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function generateId(): string {\\\\\\\\n  return Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Import a document from a JSON file\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function importDocument(file: File): Promise&lt;Document&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const reader = new FileReader();\\\\\\\\n    reader.onload = (e) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      try {\\\\\\\\n        const doc = JSON.parse(e.target?.result as string) as Document;\\\\\\\\n        // Generate new ID to avoid conflicts\\\\\\\\n        doc.id = generateId();\\\\\\\\n        doc.updatedAt = Date.now();\\\\\\\\n        resolve(doc);\\\\\\\\n      } catch (error) {\\\\\\\\n        reject(new Error('Invalid document format'));\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    reader.onerror = () =&gt; reject(reader.error);\\\\\\\\n    reader.readAsText(file);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":123}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"// boolean;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface LoginCredentials {\\\\\\\\n  username: string;\\\\\\\\n  password: string;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * 🔧 BACKEND TODO: Implement actual login API\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * This function should:\\\\\\\\n * 1. Send credentials to your authentication server\\\\\\\\n * 2. Validate username and password\\\\\\\\n * 3. Return user data and auth token on success\\\\\\\\n * 4. setPendingDeleteId] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);\\\\\\\\n  const [isDeleteDialogOpen, setIsDeleteDialogOpen] = useState(false);\\\\\\\\n  const [isDeleting, setIsDeleting] = React.ChangeEvent&lt;HTMLInputElement&gt;) {\\\\\\\\n    const file = event.target.files?.[0];\\\\\\\\n    if (!file) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Validate file type\\\\\\\\n    if (!isSupportedDocumentFile(file)) {\\\\\\\\n      toast.error('Please upload a .txt or .docx file');\\\\\\\\n      event.target.value = '';\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    try {\\\\\\\\n      // Show loading toast\\\\\\\\n      const loadingToast = toast.loading(&#96;Importing ${file.name}...&#96;);\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      // Import the document\\\\\\\\n      const newDoc = await importDocumentFile(file);\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      // Save to database\\\\\\\\n      await saveDocument(newDoc);\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      // Reload documents list\\\\\\\\n      await loadDocuments();\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      // Dismiss loading toast and show success\\\\\\\\n      toast.dismiss(loadingToast);\\\\\\\\n      toast.success(&#96;${newDoc.title} imported successfully!&#96;);\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      // Open the newly imported document\\\\\\\\n      onSelectDocument(newDoc.id);\\\\\\\\n    } catch (error) {\\\\\\\\n      console.error('Error importing document:', error);\\\\\\\\n      toast.error(error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to import document');\\\\\\\\n    } finally {\\\\\\\\n      // Reset file input\\\\\\\\n      event.target.value = '';\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  function handleDelete(id: string, event: React.MouseEvent) {\\\\\\\\n    event.stopPropagation();\\\\\\\\n    setPendingDeleteId(id);\\\\\\\\n    setIsDeleteDialogOpen(true);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  async function confirmDelete() {\\\\\\\\n    if (!pendingDeleteId) return;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n Document import utilities for GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\nimport mammoth from 'mammoth';\\\\\\\\nimport { Document, generateId } from './db';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Import a .txt file\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nasync function importTxtFile(file: File): Promise&lt;Document&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const reader = new FileReader();\\\\\\\\n    reader.onload = (e) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      try {\\\\\\\\n        const text = e.target?.result as string;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Extract filename without extension as title\\\\\\\\n        const title = file.name.replace(/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\.txt$/i, '');\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Convert plain text to HTML paragraphs\\\\\\\\n        const paragraphs = text.split('\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n').filter(line =&gt; line.trim());\\\\\\\\n        const content = paragraphs.length &gt; 0 \\\\\\\\n          ? paragraphs.map(p =&gt; &#96;&lt;div&gt;${p}&lt;/div&gt;&#96;).join('')\\\\\\\\n          : '&lt;div&gt;&lt;br&gt;&lt;/div&gt;';\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        const doc: Document = {\\\\\\\\n          id: generateId(),\\\\\\\\n          title: title || 'Untitled Document',\\\\\\\\n          content: content,\\\\\\\\n          language: 'en', // Default to English\\\\\\\\n          createdAt: Date.now(),\\\\\\\\n          updatedAt: Date.now(),\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        resolve(doc);\\\\\\\\n      } catch (error) {\\\\\\\\n        reject(new Error('Failed to read text file'));\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    reader.onerror = () =&gt; reject(reader.error);\\\\\\\\n    reader.readAsText(file);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Import a .docx file\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nasync function importDocxFile(file: File): Promise&lt;Document&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const reader = new FileReader();\\\\\\\\n    reader.onload = async (e) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      try {\\\\\\\\n        const arrayBuffer = e.target?.result as ArrayBuffer;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Use mammoth to extract HTML from .docx\\\\\\\\n        const result = await mammoth.convertToHtml({ arrayBuffer });\\\\\\\\n        let html = result.value;\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Extract filename without extension as title\\\\\\\\n        const title = file.name.replace(/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\.docx$/i, '');\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // Clean up the HTML and ensure proper structure\\\\\\\\n        // Replace &lt;p&gt; tags with &lt;div&gt; for consistency with our editor\\\\\\\\n        html = html.replace(/&lt;p&gt;/g, '&lt;div&gt;').replace(/&lt;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\/p&gt;/g, '&lt;/div&gt;');\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        // If empty, add a default empty div\\\\\\\\n        if (!html.trim()) {\\\\\\\\n          html = '&lt;div&gt;&lt;br&gt;&lt;/div&gt;';\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        const doc: Document = {\\\\\\\\n          id: generateId(),\\\\\\\\n          title: title || 'Untitled Document',\\\\\\\\n          content: html,\\\\\\\\n          language: 'en', // Default to English\\\\\\\\n          createdAt: Date.now(),\\\\\\\\n          updatedAt: Date.now(),\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\n        resolve(doc);\\\\\\\\n      } catch (error) {\\\\\\\\n        reject(new Error('Failed to read DOCX file'));\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    reader.onerror = () =&gt; reject(reader.error);\\\\\\\\n    reader.readAsArrayBuffer(file);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Import a document file (.txt or .docx)\\\\\\\\n * Creates a new document with the content from the file\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function importDocumentFile(file: File): Promise&lt;Document&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const fileName = file.name.toLowerCase();\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  if (fileName.endsWith('.txt')) {\\\\\\\\n    return importTxtFile(file);\\\\\\\\n  } else if (fileName.endsWith('.docx')) {\\\\\\\\n    return importDocxFile(file);\\\\\\\\n  } else {\\\\\\\\n    throw new Error('Unsupported file format. Please upload a .txt or .docx file.');\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Validate if a file is a supported document format\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function isSupportedDocumentFile(file: File): boolean {\\\\\\\\n  const fileName = file.name.toLowerCase();\\\\\\\\n  return fileName.endsWith('.txt') || fileName.endsWith('.docx');\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":111}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/import.ts\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"// Document import utilities for GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\nimport mammoth from 'mammoth';\\\\\\\\nimport { Document, generateId } from './db';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Import a .txt file\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nasync function importTxtFile(file: File): Promise&lt;Document&gt; GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is a browser-based document editor inspired by Google Docs, with a product focus on better multilingual writing workflows.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe current codebase is a working local-first UI prototype with rich text editing, document storage in the browser, and language-aware UX foundations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What The Project Is\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs aims to make multilingual writing feel native instead of bolted on. The core concept is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Let users choose language intent per document.\\\\\\\\n- Build toward integrated non-Latin input methods (starting with Russian in planned stories).\\\\\\\\n- Preserve a familiar docs-style experience (document list, editor, autosave, export/import).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Implementation (In This Repo)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Rich text editor built on &#96;contentEditable&#96; with formatting controls:\\\\\\\\n  - Bold, italic, underline\\\\\\\\n  - Font size, alignment, and line spacing\\\\\\\\n  - Image insertion (PNG/JPEG, with size cycling)\\\\\\\\n- Document management UI:\\\\\\\\n  - Create new document\\\\\\\\n  - Open existing document\\\\\\\\n  - Delete document\\\\\\\\n  - Edit document title\\\\\\\\n- Persistence:\\\\\\\\n  - Browser IndexedDB storage (&#96;GlossaDocs&#96; database)\\\\\\\\n  - Auto-save after 10 seconds of unsaved changes\\\\\\\\n  - Manual save button and saved/unsaved status indicators\\\\\\\\n- Import/Export:\\\\\\\\n  - Import &#96;.txt&#96; and &#96;.docx&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Export &#96;.txt&#96;, &#96;.docx&#96;, and &#96;.pdf&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Language support (current UI):\\\\\\\\n  - Per-document language selector for &#96;en&#96;, &#96;de&#96;, &#96;ru&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Direction handling scaffold exists for future RTL languages\\\\\\\\n- Auth state:\\\\\\\\n  - Login/guest flow is currently simulated with local storage placeholders (no real backend auth yet)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Tech Stack\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- React + TypeScript\\\\\\\\n- Vite\\\\\\\\n- IndexedDB for local document persistence\\\\\\\\n- UI libraries: MUI, Radix, Tailwind ecosystem utilities\\\\\\\\n- File conversion/export libraries:\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;mammoth&#96; for &#96;.docx&#96; import\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;docx&#96; and &#96;jspdf&#96; for export\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Running Locally\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm install&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Start the dev server:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm run dev&#96;\\\\\\\\n3. Open in browser:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;http://localhost:5173/&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNote: this repo is configured to run Vite with host exposure so local browser access works from &#96;npm run dev&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Limitations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- No backend or real authentication yet.\\\\\\\\n- No collaboration/sharing yet.\\\\\\\\n- Language features are in progress; current language selection is foundational, with advanced input-method behavior planned.\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":65}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/README.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is a browser-based document editor inspired by Google Docs, with a product focus on better multilingual writing workflows.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe current codebase is a working local-first UI prototype with rich text editing, document storage in the browser, and language-aware UX foundations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What The Project Is\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs aims to make multilingual writing feel native instead of bolted on. The core concept is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Let users choose language intent per document.\\\\\\\\n- Build toward integrated non-Latin input methods (starting with Russian in planned stories).\\\\\\\\n- Preserve a familiar docs-style experience (document list, editor, autosave, export/import).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Implementation (In This Repo)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Rich text editor built on &#96;contentEditable&#96; with formatting controls:\\\\\\\\n  - Bold, italic, underline\\\\\\\\n  - Font size, alignment, and line spacing\\\\\\\\n  - Image insertion (PNG/JPEG, with size cycling)\\\\\\\\n- Document management UI:\\\\\\\\n  - Create new document\\\\\\\\n  - Open existing document\\\\\\\\n  - Delete document\\\\\\\\n  - Edit document title\\\\\\\\n- Persistence:\\\\\\\\n  - Browser IndexedDB storage (&#96;GlossaDocs&#96; database)\\\\\\\\n  - Auto-save after 10 seconds of unsaved changes\\\\\\\\n  - Manual save button and saved/unsaved status indicators\\\\\\\\n- Import/Export:\\\\\\\\n  - Import &#96;.txt&#96; and &#96;.docx&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Export &#96;.txt&#96;, &#96;.docx&#96;, and &#96;.pdf&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Language support (current UI):\\\\\\\\n  - Per-document language selector for &#96;en&#96;, &#96;de&#96;, &#96;ru&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Direction handling scaffold exists for future RTL languages\\\\\\\\n- Auth state:\\\\\\\\n  - Login/guest flow is currently simulated with local storage placeholders (no real backend auth yet)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Tech Stack\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- React + TypeScript\\\\\\\\n- Vite\\\\\\\\n- IndexedDB for local document persistence\\\\\\\\n- UI libraries: MUI, Radix, Tailwind ecosystem utilities\\\\\\\\n- File conversion/export libraries:\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;mammoth&#96; for &#96;.docx&#96; import\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;docx&#96; and &#96;jspdf&#96; for export\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Running Locally\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm install&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Start the dev server:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm run dev&#96;\\\\\\\\n3. Open in browser:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;http://localhost:5173/&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNote: this repo is configured to run Vite with host exposure so local browser access works from &#96;npm run dev&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Limitations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- No backend or real authentication yet.\\\\\\\\n- No collaboration/sharing yet.\\\\\\\\n- Language features are in progress; current language selection is foundational, with advanced input-method behavior **Frontend framework + key libraries**\\\\n  - **React + TypeScript + Vite**: app bootstraps with &#96;createRoot(...).render(&lt;App /&gt;)&#96; in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/main.tsx&#96;; Vite config uses &#96;@vitejs/plugin-react&#96; in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/vite.config.ts&#96;.\\\\n  - **UI stack**: heavy Radix UI primitives, Tailwind (&#96;tailwindcss&#96;, &#96;@tailwindcss/vite&#96;), plus component utilities (&#96;class-variance-authority&#96;, &#96;clsx&#96;, &#96;tailwind-merge&#96;) in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/package.json&#96;.\\\\n  - **Other notable libs in active code**: &#96;sonner&#96; (toasts), &#96;lucide-react&#96; (icons), &#96;motion&#96; (&#96;AnimatePresence&#96; in login), &#96;mammoth&#96; (DOCX import), &#96;docx&#96; + &#96;html2pdf.js&#96;/&#96;jspdf&#96; (export), all declared in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/package.json&#96; and used from &#96;src/app/components&#96; / &#96;src/app/utils&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n- **Where documents are stored (IndexedDB / browser storage)**\\\\n  - **IndexedDB (primary doc storage)**: DB name &#96;GlossaDocs&#96;, object store &#96;documents&#96;, keyPath &#96;id&#96;, index &#96;updatedAt&#96; in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts&#96; (&#96;initDB&#96;, &#96;getAllDocuments&#96;, &#96;getDocument&#96;, &#96;saveDocument&#96;, &#96;deleteDocument&#96;).\\\\n  - **Read/write call sites**:\\\\n    - List/load/delete/import-save in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/components/DocumentList.tsx&#96;.\\\\n    - Editor load/save in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/components/Editor.tsx&#96;.\\\\n  - **localStorage (auth/session only, not docs)**: keys &#96;glossadocs_user&#96; and &#96;authToken&#96; in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/auth.ts&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n- **Current document data model**\\\\n  - &#96;Document&#96; interface in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts&#96;:\\\\n    - &#96;id: string&#96;\\\\n    - &#96;title: string&#96;\\\\n    - &#96;content: string&#96; (HTML content from &#96;contentEditable&#96;)\\\\n    - &#96;language: 'en' | 'de' | 'ru'&#96;\\\\n    - &#96;createdAt: number&#96;\\\\n    - &#96;updatedAt: number&#96;\\\\n  - New-doc defaults are created in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/components/Editor.tsx&#96; (empty content, default title/language, timestamps).\\\\n  - Import paths (&#96;.txt&#96;, &#96;.docx&#96;) map into the same model in &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/import.ts&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n- **Existing API client / backend assumptions**\\\\n  - No real API client currently: no active &#96;fetch&#96;/&#96;axios&#96; network layer found for app data/auth flows.\\\\n  - &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/auth.ts&#96; is explicitly marked placeholder with “BACKEND INTEGRATION NEEDED”; login/guest/logout are simulated with &#96;setTimeout&#96; + &#96;localStorage&#96;.\\\\n  - Backend expectation is documented in comments there (example &#96;fetch('https://your-api.com/auth/login', ...)&#96;) but not implemented.\\\\n  - &#96;C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/README.md&#96; confirms local-first prototype and “no backend or real GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is a browser-based document editor inspired by Google Docs, with a product focus on better multilingual writing workflows.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe current codebase is a working local-first UI prototype with rich text editing, document storage in the browser, and language-aware UX foundations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What The Project Is\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs aims to make multilingual writing feel native instead of bolted on. The core concept is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Let users choose language intent per document.\\\\\\\\n- Build toward integrated non-Latin input methods (starting with Russian in planned stories).\\\\\\\\n- Preserve a familiar docs-style experience (document list, editor, autosave, export/import).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Implementation (In This Repo)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Rich text editor built on &#96;contentEditable&#96; with formatting controls:\\\\\\\\n  - Bold, italic, underline\\\\\\\\n  - Font size, alignment, and line spacing\\\\\\\\n  - Image insertion (PNG/JPEG, with size cycling)\\\\\\\\n- Document management UI:\\\\\\\\n  - Create new document\\\\\\\\n  - Open existing document\\\\\\\\n  - Delete document\\\\\\\\n  - Edit document title\\\\\\\\n- Persistence:\\\\\\\\n  - Browser IndexedDB storage (&#96;GlossaDocs&#96; database)\\\\\\\\n  - Auto-save after 10 seconds of unsaved changes\\\\\\\\n  - Manual save button and saved/unsaved status indicators\\\\\\\\n- Import/Export:\\\\\\\\n  - Import &#96;.txt&#96; and &#96;.docx&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Export &#96;.txt&#96;, &#96;.docx&#96;, and &#96;.pdf&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Language support (current UI):\\\\\\\\n  - Per-document language selector for &#96;en&#96;, &#96;de&#96;, &#96;ru&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Direction handling scaffold exists for future RTL languages\\\\\\\\n- Auth state:\\\\\\\\n  - Login/guest flow is currently simulated with local storage placeholders (no real backend auth yet)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Tech Stack\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- React + TypeScript\\\\\\\\n- Vite\\\\\\\\n- IndexedDB for local document persistence\\\\\\\\n- UI libraries: MUI, Radix, Tailwind ecosystem utilities\\\\\\\\n- File conversion/export libraries:\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;mammoth&#96; for &#96;.docx&#96; import\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;docx&#96; and &#96;jspdf&#96; for export\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Running Locally\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm install&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Start the dev server:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm run dev&#96;\\\\\\\\n3. Open in browser:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;http://localhost:5173/&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNote: this repo is configured to run Vite with host exposure so local browser access works from &#96;npm run dev&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Limitations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- No backend or real authentication yet.\\\\\\\\n- No collaboration/sharing yet.\\\\\\\\n- Language features are in progress; current language selection is foundational, with advanced input-method behavior planned.\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":65}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/README.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is a browser-based document editor inspired by Google Docs, with a product focus on better multilingual writing workflows.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe current codebase is a working local-first UI prototype with rich text editing, document storage in the browser, and language-aware UX foundations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What The Project Is\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs aims to make multilingual writing feel native instead of bolted on. The core concept is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Let users choose language intent per document.\\\\\\\\n- Build toward integrated non-Latin input methods (starting with Russian in planned stories).\\\\\\\\n- Preserve a familiar docs-style experience (document list, editor, autosave, export/import).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Implementation (In This Repo)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Rich text editor built on &#96;contentEditable&#96; with formatting controls:\\\\\\\\n  - Bold, italic, underline\\\\\\\\n  - Font size, alignment, and line spacing\\\\\\\\n  - Image insertion (PNG/JPEG, with size cycling)\\\\\\\\n- Document management UI:\\\\\\\\n  - Create new document\\\\\\\\n  - Open existing document\\\\\\\\n  - Delete document\\\\\\\\n  - Edit document title\\\\\\\\n- Persistence:\\\\\\\\n  - Browser IndexedDB storage (&#96;GlossaDocs&#96; database)\\\\\\\\n  - Auto-save after 10 seconds of unsaved changes\\\\\\\\n  - Manual save button and saved/unsaved status indicators\\\\\\\\n- Import/Export:\\\\\\\\n  - Import &#96;.txt&#96; and &#96;.docx&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Export &#96;.txt&#96;, &#96;.docx&#96;, and &#96;.pdf&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Language support (current UI):\\\\\\\\n  - Per-document language selector for &#96;en&#96;, &#96;de&#96;, &#96;ru&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Direction handling scaffold exists for future RTL languages\\\\\\\\n- Auth state:\\\\\\\\n  - Login/guest flow is currently simulated with local storage placeholders (no real backend auth yet)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Tech Stack\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- React + TypeScript\\\\\\\\n- Vite\\\\\\\\n- IndexedDB for local document persistence\\\\\\\\n- UI libraries: MUI, Radix, Tailwind ecosystem utilities\\\\\\\\n- File conversion/export libraries:\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;mammoth&#96; for &#96;.docx&#96; import\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;docx&#96; and &#96;jspdf&#96; for export\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Running Locally\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm install&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Start the dev server:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm run dev&#96;\\\\\\\\n3. Open in browser:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;http://localhost:5173/&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNote: this repo is configured to run Vite with host exposure so local browser access works from &#96;npm run dev&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Limitations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- No backend or real authentication yet.\\\\\\\\n- No collaboration/sharing yet.\\\\\\\\n- Language features are in progress; current * Authentication utilities for GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * ⚠️ BACKEND INTEGRATION NEEDED ⚠️\\\\\\\\n * These are placeholder functions that simulate authentication.\\\\\\\\n * Replace these with actual backend API calls when ready.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface User {\\\\\\\\n  id: string;\\\\\\\\n  username: string;\\\\\\\\n  email?: string;\\\\\\\\n  isGuest: boolean;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface LoginCredentials {\\\\\\\\n  username: string;\\\\\\\\n  password: string;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * 🔧 BACKEND TODO: Implement actual login API\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * This function should:\\\\\\\\n * 1. Send credentials to your authentication server\\\\\\\\n * 2. Validate username and password\\\\\\\\n * 3. Return user data and auth token on success\\\\\\\\n * 4. Handle errors (invalid credentials, server errors, etc.)\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * Example implementation:\\\\\\\\n * &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n * const response = await fetch('https://your-api.com/auth/login', {\\\\\\\\n *   method: 'POST',\\\\\\\\n *   headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\\\\\\\\n *   body: JSON.stringify(credentials)\\\\\\\\n * });\\\\\\\\n * const data = await response.json();\\\\\\\\n * if (!response.ok) throw new Error(data.message);\\\\\\\\n * localStorage.setItem('authToken', data.token);\\\\\\\\n * return data.user;\\\\\\\\n * &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function loginWithCredentials(\\\\\\\\n  credentials: LoginCredentials\\\\\\\\n): Promise&lt;User&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  // Simulate API call delay\\\\\\\\n  await new Promise((resolve) =&gt; setTimeout(resolve, 800));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // PLACEHOLDER: Replace with actual API call\\\\\\\\n  if (credentials.username &amp;&amp; credentials.password) {\\\\\\\\n    // Simulate successful login\\\\\\\\n    const user: User = {\\\\\\\\n      id: &#96;user_${Date.now()}&#96;,\\\\\\\\n      username: credentials.username,\\\\\\\\n      email: &#96;${credentials.username}@example.com&#96;,\\\\\\\\n      isGuest: false,\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Store user in localStorage (replace with proper token storage)\\\\\\\\n    localStorage.setItem('glossadocs_user', JSON.stringify(user));\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    return user;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  throw new Error('Invalid credentials');\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * 🔧 BACKEND TODO: Generate guest session\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * This function should:\\\\\\\\n * 1. Create a temporary guest session on the server\\\\\\\\n * 2. Return a guest user object with limited permissions\\\\\\\\n * 3. Optionally set a guest token for tracking\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function continueAsGuest(): Promise&lt;User&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  // Simulate API call delay\\\\\\\\n  await new Promise((resolve) =&gt; setTimeout(resolve, 500));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // PLACEHOLDER: Replace with actual API call\\\\\\\\n  const guestUser: User = {\\\\\\\\n    id: &#96;guest_${Date.now()}&#96;,\\\\\\\\n    username: 'Guest',\\\\\\\\n    isGuest: true,\\\\\\\\n  };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // Store guest user in localStorage\\\\\\\\n  localStorage.setItem('glossadocs_user', JSON.stringify(guestUser));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  return guestUser;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * 🔧 BACKEND TODO: Implement logout API\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * This function should:\\\\\\\\n * 1. Invalidate the user's session on the server\\\\\\\\n * 2. Clear authentication tokens\\\\\\\\n * 3. Clean up any user-specific data\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function logout(): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  // PLACEHOLDER: Replace with actual API call\\\\\\\\n  localStorage.removeItem('glossadocs_user');\\\\\\\\n  localStorage.removeItem('authToken');\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Get the currently logged-in user from local storage\\\\\\\\n * In production, validate the token with the backend\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function getCurrentUser(): User | null {\\\\\\\\n  try {\\\\\\\\n    const userJson = localStorage.getItem('glossadocs_user');\\\\\\\\n    if (!userJson) return null;\\\\\\\\n    return JSON.parse(userJson);\\\\\\\\n  } catch {\\\\\\\\n    return null;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Check if user is authenticated\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function isAuthenticated(): boolean {\\\\\\\\n  return getCurrentUser() !== null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":133}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/auth.ts\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/**\\\\\\\\n * Authentication utilities for GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * ⚠️ BACKEND INTEGRATION NEEDED ⚠️\\\\\\\\n * These are placeholder functions that simulate authentication.\\\\\\\\n * Replace these with actual backend API calls when ready.\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface User {\\\\\\\\n  id: string;\\\\\\\\n  username: string;\\\\\\\\n  email?: string;\\\\\\\\n  isGuest: boolean;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface LoginCredentials {\\\\\\\\n  username: string;\\\\\\\\n  password: string;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * 🔧 BACKEND TODO: Implement actual login API\\\\\\\\n * ============================================\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * This function should:\\\\\\\\n * 1. IndexedDB utilities for GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface Document {\\\\\\\\n  id: string;\\\\\\\\n  title: string;\\\\\\\\n  content: string;\\\\\\\\n  language: 'en' | 'de' | 'ru';\\\\\\\\n  createdAt: number;\\\\\\\\n  updatedAt: number;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst DB_NAME = 'GlossaDocs';\\\\\\\\nconst STORE_NAME = 'documents';\\\\\\\\nconst DB_VERSION = 1;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nlet dbInstance: IDBDatabase | null = null;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Initialize the IndexedDB database\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function initDB(): Promise&lt;IDBDatabase&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  if (dbInstance) {\\\\\\\\n    return dbInstance;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onerror = () =&gt; reject(request.error);\\\\\\\\n    request.onsuccess = () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      dbInstance = request.result;\\\\\\\\n      resolve(request.result);\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onupgradeneeded = (event) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      const db = (event.target as IDBOpenDBRequest).result;\\\\\\\\n      if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(STORE_NAME)) {\\\\\\\\n        const objectStore = db.createObjectStore(STORE_NAME, { keyPath: 'id' });\\\\\\\\n        objectStore.createIndex('updatedAt', 'updatedAt', { unique: false });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Helper function to execute an IndexedDB transaction\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nasync function executeTransaction&lt;T&gt;(\\\\\\\\n  mode: IDBTransactionMode,\\\\\\\\n  operation: (store: IDBObjectStore) =&gt; IDBRequest&lt;T&gt;\\\\\\\\n): Promise&lt;T&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const db = await initDB();\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const transaction = db.transaction([STORE_NAME], mode);\\\\\\\\n    const objectStore = transaction.objectStore(STORE_NAME);\\\\\\\\n    const request = operation(objectStore);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onerror = () =&gt; reject(request.error);\\\\\\\\n    request.onsuccess = () =&gt; resolve(request.result);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Get all documents sorted by most recently updated\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function getAllDocuments(): Promise&lt;Document[]&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const docs = await executeTransaction&lt;Document[]&gt;('readonly', (store) =&gt; store.getAll());\\\\\\\\n  // Sort by most recently updated\\\\\\\\n  return docs.sort((a, b) =&gt; b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Get a single document by ID\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function getDocument(id: string): Promise&lt;Document | null&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const result = await executeTransaction&lt;Document | undefined&gt;('readonly', (store) =&gt;\\\\\\\\n    store.get(id)\\\\\\\\n  );\\\\\\\\n  return result || null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Save or update a document\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function saveDocument(doc: Document): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  await executeTransaction&lt;IDBValidKey&gt;('readwrite', (store) =&gt; store.put(doc));\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Delete a document by ID\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function deleteDocument(id: string): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  await executeTransaction&lt;undefined&gt;('readwrite', (store) =&gt; store.delete(id));\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Generate a unique ID for a document\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function generateId(): string {\\\\\\\\n  return Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Import a document from a JSON file\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function importDocument(file: File): Promise&lt;Document&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const reader = new FileReader();\\\\\\\\n    reader.onload = (e) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      try {\\\\\\\\n        const doc = JSON.parse(e.target?.result as string) as Document;\\\\\\\\n        // Generate new ID to avoid conflicts\\\\\\\\n        doc.id = generateId();\\\\\\\\n        doc.updatedAt = Date.now();\\\\\\\\n        resolve(doc);\\\\\\\\n      } catch (error) {\\\\\\\\n        reject(new Error('Invalid document format'));\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    reader.onerror = () =&gt; reject(reader.error);\\\\\\\\n    reader.readAsText(file);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":123}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"// );\\\\\\\\n  return result || null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Save or update a document\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function saveDocument(doc: Document): Promise&lt;void&gt; paceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"call_4aeqMIdl4MEEkQZOzkITeZ2h\\\\nfc_091643f4fac5d3f40069a71108c48c8195b7a26b1794a642a7\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"call_4aeqMIdl4MEEkQZOzkITeZ2h\\\\nfc_091643f4fac5d3f40069a71108c48c8195b7a26b1794a642a7\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/logs/gpt/requirements-eng/value-proposition.txt\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/logs/gpt/requirements-eng/value-proposition.txt\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/logs/gpt/requirements-eng/value-proposition.txt\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"This is a description of a project I'm about to undertake. To the best of your knowledge, is my last sentence correct?\\\\\\\\nThe one-sentence description of my project, which I’ve decided to call GlossaDocs, is “Google Docs, but with better multi-language support”. I’ve taken many language classes throughout my schooling, and most of them have not used the Latin alphabet. Throughout six years of Greek, two years of Russian, and a year of Swedish, I’ve had to type a lot of characters that do not appear on a standard English keyboard, and never had a convenient way to do it. To be clear, this is not an unsolved problem. You can download alternate keyboards layouts, but this requires you to learn a whole new keyboard without any visual aid. There are also numerous websites that allow you to use non-English keyboards and paste that text into your document software, but they are all slightly clunky and aren’t integrated into the document software.\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs will be manifestly different in two main ways. First, integrated keyboards will allow users to type nonstandard English characters without having to download a separate application or go to another website. Second, users will be able to choose what language they intend to type in, and GlossaDocs’ spell-check and grammar functions will intelligently switch based on that choice. As far as I know, there is no document software (and certainly no free one) that gives people these options, making GlossaDocs a unique and valuable tool for many different types of users.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGreat project idea—and the motivation is very clearly explained. 👍\\\\\\\\nOn the idea itself: totally solid. On the last sentence specifically: it’s not quite correct, and it’s worth tightening to avoid an easy “well actually” from a reviewer.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhere the last sentence overreaches\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis part is the issue:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n“As far as I know, there is no document software (and certainly no free one) that gives people these options …”\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nBoth of the features you list do exist today, even in free tools:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGoogle Docs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHas an on-screen virtual keyboard for many languages/scripts (via Tools → Preferences → Substitutions or the Input Tools extension / built-in input methods).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSupports language-specific spell check, and users can assign different languages to different text selections.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nLibreOffice Writer (free, open source)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSupports multiple keyboard layouts and input methods.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHas per-language spellchecking and grammar tools.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMicrosoft Word\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHas language-aware spellcheck and supports alternative input methods (though it’s not free).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo a technically savvy reader will likely push back on the claim of nonexistence, especially singling out free software.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhat is still very defensible (and strong)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYour core differentiation is still compelling, just more nuanced:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExisting tools:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHide these features\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMake them clunky to access\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTreat multilingual typing as an edge case\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYour vision:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMakes multilingual typing first-class\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIntegrates keyboards directly and visually\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOptimizes the workflow for people who switch languages frequently\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThat’s a much stronger—and harder to refute—position.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHow I’d suggest rephrasing the last sentence\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHere are a few options depending on how bold you want to be:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSafer and accurate\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAs far as I know, no widely used document editor makes multilingual typing this seamless or treats integrated language-aware input as a first-class feature, especially in a free, web-based tool.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nStill confident, but harder to poke holes in\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhile some existing document editors support multilingual input in limited or hidden ways, GlossaDocs is designed from the ground up to make integrated keyboards and language-aware editing central to the writing experience.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMore visionary\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs aims to fill the gap between “technically possible” and “pleasant to use” for multilingual writers, making features that exist today feel natural, visible, and effortless.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you want, I can also help you:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nsharpen this into a one-paragraph pitch\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nrewrite it for a proposal / README\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nor stress-test it the way a skeptical engineer or professor might 😄\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGive a one or two sentence value proposition(s) for this product\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHere are a few tight options, depending on the vibe you want:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nClear and practical\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs makes multilingual writing effortless by embedding language-specific keyboards and spellchecking directly into your document. No extensions, no copy-paste, no switching apps—just write in the language you’re thinking in.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nUser-pain focused\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is a document editor built for people who write in more than one language. It removes the friction of special characters and language switching so multilingual writing feels as natural as typing in English.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMore bold / product-vision\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs treats multilingual writing as a first-class experience, not an afterthought. Integrated keyboards and automatic language-aware editing let writers focus on ideas, not input hacks.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nShort and punchy\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is Google Docs for multilingual writers—integrated keyboards, language-aware editing, and zero setup.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you want, I can tune this for a landing page hero, a grant/proposal, or a technical README tone.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nPick the one you think is best\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nI’d go with this one:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs treats multilingual writing as a first-class experience, not an afterthought. Integrated keyboards and automatic language-aware editing let writers focus on ideas, not input hacks.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhy this works best:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIt doesn’t overclaim or invite nitpicky comparisons\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIt clearly signals who it’s for (multilingual writers)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIt emphasizes philosophy + benefit, not just features\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIt still leaves room for future expansion beyond keyboards\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you want a slightly punchier version for a landing page hero, I can trim it to a single sentence without losing that strength.\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":118}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/logs/gpt/requirements-eng/value-proposition.txt\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"This is a description of a project I'm about to undertake. To the best of your knowledge, is my last sentence correct?\\\\\\\\nThe one-sentence description of my project, which I’ve decided to call GlossaDocs, is “Google Docs, but with better multi-language support”. I’ve taken many language classes throughout my schooling, and most of them have not used the Latin alphabet. Throughout six years of Greek, two years of Russian, and a year of Swedish, I’ve had to type a lot of characters that do not appear on a standard English keyboard, and never had a convenient way to do it. To be clear, this is not an unsolved problem. You can download alternate keyboards layouts, but this requires you to learn a whole new keyboard without any visual aid. There are also numerous websites that allow you to use non-English keyboards and paste that text into your document software, but they are all slightly clunky and aren’t integrated into the document software.\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs will be manifestly different in two main ways. First, integrated keyboards will allow users to type nonstandard English characters without having to download a separate application or go to another website. Second, users will be able to choose what language they intend to type in, and GlossaDocs’ spell-check and grammar functions will intelligently switch based on that choice. As far as I know, there is no document software (and certainly no free one) that gives people these options, making GlossaDocs a unique and valuable tool for many different types of users.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGreat project idea—and the motivation is very clearly explained. 👍\\\\\\\\nOn the idea itself: totally solid. On the last sentence specifically: it’s not quite correct, and it’s worth tightening to avoid an easy “well actually” from a reviewer.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhere the last sentence overreaches\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis part is the issue:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n“As far as I know, there is no document software (and certainly no free one) that gives people these options …”\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nBoth of the features you list do exist today, even in free tools:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGoogle Docs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHas an on-screen virtual keyboard for many languages/scripts (via Tools → Preferences → Substitutions or the Input Tools extension / built-in input methods).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSupports language-specific spell check, and users can assign different languages to different text selections.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nLibreOffice Writer (free, open source)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSupports multiple keyboard layouts and input methods.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHas per-language spellchecking and grammar tools.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMicrosoft Word\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHas language-aware spellcheck and supports alternative input methods (though it’s not free).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo a technically savvy reader will likely push back on the claim of nonexistence, especially singling out free software.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhat is still very defensible (and strong)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYour core differentiation is still compelling, just more nuanced:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExisting tools:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHide these features\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMake them clunky to access\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTreat multilingual typing as an edge case\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYour vision:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMakes multilingual typing first-class\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIntegrates keyboards directly and visually\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOptimizes the workflow for people who switch languages frequently\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThat’s a much stronger—and harder to refute—position.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHow I’d suggest document editor makes multilingual typing this seamless or treats integrated language-aware input as a first-class feature, especially in a free, web-based tool.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nStill confident, but harder to poke holes in\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhile some existing document editors support multilingual input in limited or hidden ways, GlossaDocs is designed from the ground up to make integrated _**User**_

I want an email or username and password, but ideally I'm not managing the auth myself - that sounds like a recipe for insecure code. Do you think that's wrong?

---

_**Agent (model gpt-5.3-codex, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Confirming auth method preference**</details></think>

---

You’re absolutely right—avoiding custom auth logic is the safer choice, not wrong at all. For your constraints (free, local dev now, AWS later), a managed-by-standard approach like **OIDC with a dedicated identity provider** is the right direction so your app/backend only verifies tokens and handles authorization.



---

_**Agent (model gpt-5.3-codex, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Preparing architecture diagrams**</details></think>

---

Absolutely—here’s a formal architecture diagram set you can use as the implementation reference.

## System Architecture (Logical)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  user[UserBrowser] --> fe[ReactFrontendVite]
  fe -->|OIDCAuthorizeCodePKCE| kc[KeycloakIdP]
  fe -->|BearerJWT HTTPS| api[FastifyApi]

  api --> authz[AuthMiddlewareJWTValidation]
  authz --> docs[DocumentService]
  docs --> repo[DocumentRepository]
  repo --> pg[(PostgreSQL)]

  api --> jwks[JWKSCache]
  jwks --> kc

  subgraph core [CoreDomains]
    docs
    repo
  end
```

## Deployment Architecture (Local + AWS)

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph local [LocalDevDocker]
    feLocal[ReactFrontendDevServer]
    apiLocal[FastifyApiContainer]
    kcLocal[KeycloakContainer]
    pgLocal[(PostgreSQLContainer)]
    feLocal --> apiLocal
    feLocal --> kcLocal
    apiLocal --> kcLocal
    apiLocal --> pgLocal
  end

  subgraph aws [AWSDeployment]
    cloudfront[CloudFrontOrALB]
    feS3[S3StaticFrontend]
    apiSvc[ECSFargateOrEC2Fastify]
    kcSvc[KeycloakOnEC2OrECS]
    rds[(RDSPostgreSQL)]
    cloudfront --> feS3
    cloudfront --> apiSvc
    feS3 --> kcSvc
    feS3 --> apiSvc
    apiSvc --> kcSvc
    apiSvc --> rds
  end
```

## Request Flow (Authenticated Document Save)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as UserBrowser
  participant FE as ReactFrontend
  participant KC as Keycloak
  participant API as FastifyApi
  participant DB as PostgreSQL

  U->>FE: Edit document
  FE->>KC: Login (OIDC PKCE)
  KC-->>FE: Access token (JWT)
  FE->>API: PUT /documents/:id (Bearer JWT)
  API->>KC: Validate JWT via JWKS
  API->>DB: Update where id and owner_id=token.sub
  DB-->>API: Success
  API-->>FE: 200 Updated
```

## Capacity Note (10 simultaneous users)

- 10 concurrent users is very feasible with this stack.
- Baseline target: 1 Fastify instance (0.25-0.5 vCPU, 512MB-1GB RAM) + small Postgres instance is sufficient for V1 CRUD load.
- Keep response times stable by adding:
  - DB indexes on `owner_id`, `updated_at`, `id`
  - connection pooling
  - JWT JWKS caching
  - API rate limiting and request validation

If you want, I can next turn this into a concrete `docs/backend-architecture.md` with component responsibilities, endpoint contracts, and a phased implementation checklist tied directly to each box in the diagram.

Created both specs in `docs/specs/` and aligned them to one shared backend architecture:

- `docs/specs/user-story-1-basic-editor-spec.md`
- `docs/specs/user-story-3-russian-onscreen-keyboard-spec.md`

What I made sure of:
- Both specs explicitly assume the same backend from `docs/backend-architecture.md`.
- Story 3 is additive on top of Story 1 (no separate backend/service silo).
- Story 1 defines document CRUD + auth-backed ownership.
- Story 3 reuses Story 1 persistence path and adds only keyboard behavior + a global `keyboard_visible` setting model.

If you want, I can now add a third “cross-story contract” doc (`docs/specs/shared-backend-contracts.md`) that locks shared entities/endpoints so future story specs stay consistent automatically.

Update the single, unified architecture for the backend that supports both user stories. Write down a text description to describe it and draw it as a Mermaid diagram. Justify your design choices as if you were speaking to a senior architect with a company

Design the internal architecture for the module. 

Write down a text description to describe it and draw it as a Mermaid diagram. Justify your design choices as if you were speaking to a senior architect.

Define the data abstraction used in the module. 

Determine the stable storage mechanism for the module (i.e. you can’t just use an in-memory data structure because GlossaDocs might crash and lose its memory)

Define any data schemas required to communicate with any storage databases.

Define a clear, unambiguous API for external callers of this module (a REST API for any services accessible over the web)

Provide a list of all class, method, and field declarations. Identify which are externally visible and which are private to the module.

Draw a Mermaid class hierarchy diagram that shows the module-internal view of each class.

---

## Findings (PR Review)

### 1) Broken internal links in architecture index (High)
The links in `docs/architecture/backend-architecture.md` are relative-path incorrect from that file’s location, so navigation breaks.

```6:12:docs/architecture/backend-architecture.md
- [System Architecture](architecture/system.md)
- [API Edge Module](architecture/modules/api-edge-module.md)
- [Identity and Access Module](architecture/modules/identity-access-module.md)
- [Document Module](architecture/modules/document-module.md)
- [Input Preferences Module](architecture/modules/input-preferences-module.md)
```

From `docs/architecture/backend-architecture.md`, those should be `system.md` and `modules/...`.

---

### 2) System diagram references a module that has no module-spec doc (High)
`OperationalStoreModule` is shown as a first-class backend module, but there is no corresponding module document in `docs/architecture/modules/`. This is a structural inconsistency in the architecture set.

```30:46:docs/architecture/system.md
subgraph app [GlossaDocsBackend]
  edge[ApiEdgeModule]
  idm[IdentityAccessModule]
  docm[DocumentModule]
  setm[InputPreferencesModule]
  obs[OperationalStoreModule]
  edge --> idm
  edge --> docm
  edge --> setm
  edge --> obs
end
```

Either add `operational-store-module.md` or fold these concerns explicitly into `api-edge-module.md` and remove the separate node.

---

### 3) Class hierarchy diagrams drop nullable return contracts (Medium)
Text declarations include nullable returns, but class diagrams remove nullability. This can mislead implementers around not-found behavior.

```59:69:docs/architecture/modules/document-module.md
public getOwned(ownerSub: string, id: string): Promise<DocumentAggregate | null>
...
public findOwnedById(ownerSub: string, id: string): Promise<DocumentRow | null>
public updateOwned(ownerSub: string, id: string, patch: PartialDocumentRow): Promise<DocumentRow | null>
```

```81:94:docs/architecture/modules/document-module.md
+getOwned(ownerSub, id) Promise~DocumentAggregate~
...
+findOwnedById(ownerSub, id) Promise~DocumentRow~
+updateOwned(ownerSub, id, patch) Promise~DocumentRow~
```

---

### 4) Identity naming inconsistency (`username` vs `preferredUsername`) (Medium)
The abstraction says `preferredUsername`, but API response spec says `username`. Minor, but this drift creates avoidable DTO mapping ambiguity.

```31:44:docs/architecture/modules/identity-access-module.md
- `AuthenticatedPrincipal { sub, preferredUsername, email?, scopes[] }`
...
- `GET /me` -> `{ sub, username, email, scopes }`
```

Choose one canonical name and stick to it through abstractions + API contracts.

---

### 5) Potential over-design for current scope in API Edge persistence (Low/Design risk)
For the current stage (10 concurrent users), durable tables for idempotency + rate counters + audit may be heavier than needed unless there’s a compliance or replay requirement now.

```39:47:docs/architecture/modules/api-edge-module.md
- `api_audit_events`
- `api_idempotency_keys`
- `api_rate_limit_counters`
```

Not wrong, but probably phase these: keep `audit` first, defer `idempotency`/`rate_limit_counters` unless required.

---

## Overall Assessment
- Architecture quality is strong and mostly coherent.
- Biggest actionable issues are **navigation break**, **missing module spec parity**, and **contract consistency (nullability + identity field names)**.
- After those are fixed, this becomes a solid implementation reference for backend build-out.

If you want, I can make these doc fixes directly in one pass.

---

**Simplifying identity naming**

I’m considering whether to keep "preferredUsername" internal or switch to username completely. I might want to modify the api-edge module for stable storage and schemas. Regarding the rate limit, I think it's better to mention it as pluggable but not strictly necessary for in-memory options. Implemented the review fixes directly across `docs/architecture` and cleaned the main issues I flagged.

### Changes made

- **Fixed broken index links** in `docs/architecture/backend-architecture.md`
  - Paths now resolve correctly from the `docs/architecture` folder.
  - Added missing module link for operational concerns.

- **Added missing module spec**
  - New file: `docs/architecture/modules/operational-store-module.md`
  - Includes: features/non-features, internal diagram, data abstractions, storage schemas, external API (none), class/method declarations, class diagram.

- **Reduced over-engineering in API edge persistence**
  - Updated `docs/architecture/modules/api-edge-module.md` to use a phased approach:
    - required now: `api_audit_events`
    - optional later: `api_idempotency_keys`
    - no mandatory DB persistence for rate limiting in V1

- **Aligned identity contract naming**
  - Updated `docs/architecture/modules/identity-access-module.md`
  - `AuthenticatedPrincipal` now consistently uses `username` (matching `/me` response).

- **Normalized nullability in class diagram contracts**
  - Updated `docs/architecture/modules/document-module.md` class diagram to explicitly represent nullable returns (`NullableDocumentAggregate`, `NullableDocumentRow`) so it matches the text API contract.

### PR-style status

- **High-priority inconsistencies:** resolved
- **Medium contract drift issues:** resolved
- **Complexity concerns:** reduced with phased ops strategy

If you want, I can do one more pass focused only on **terminology normalization** (for example: `ownerSub` vs `owner_id` vs `actorSub`) to make naming fully uniform across all architecture docs.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Standardizing naming conventions**

I need to ensure consistent naming across the system. Currently, I'm using "actorSub" for authenticated user IDs in code-level abstractions, while "ownerSub" is being used in document and settings modules. It might be better to standardize to "actorSub" everywhere. Also, I might want to adjust the system so "owner_id" aligns with "actorSub." I should consider updating method parameters in the document and input modules too. I think adding a Naming Conventions section would help clarify these changes. I'll need to edit various files to implement this.
**Clarifying API identity standards**

I need to clarify the naming conventions regarding identity in the API. Currently, I have "sub" tied to identity but might want to unify it under "actorSub," which is derived from the sub claim. I could update features to describe the authenticated principal as including `actorSub`, username, and scopes. It might be unconventional for the `/me` endpoint to return `actorSub`, but I should keep `sub` for consistency with OIDC standards. A standard table would help clarify this, using `sub` for external APIs, `actorSub` for internal references, and `owner_id` for domain storage.
**Deciding on parameter naming**

I'm considering whether to mention mapping explicitly and whether to use "ownerSub" or "actorSub" for method parameters. I think it would be clearer to standardize on "actorSub" for service or repo methods, reflecting the caller's principal. The document service currently uses "ownerSub," so I need to adjust that, along with input settings. In the identity module, I might lean towards "actorSub" for internal variables. I’ll patch multiple files one at a time, starting with adding naming conventions and updating ownership checks. I’ll make sure to clarify the use of "owner_id" consistently as "actorSub."
**Clarifying API request conventions**

I'm considering the separation between "requestId" and "request_id" in the API edge. It seems like a good idea to mention mapping in the data abstractions to maintain clarity. I think adding a bullet point could help highlight this distinction for anyone working with the API. I’ll plan to patch this in the documentation, ensuring that everything is concise and easy to understand. It might help avoid confusion in the future!</details></think>

Completed a full naming-consistency pass across `docs/architecture` and normalized terminology.

## What I standardized

- **OIDC/external identity field:** `sub`
- **Internal runtime identity variable:** `actorSub`
- **Domain DB ownership column:** `owner_id`
- **Operational DB actor column:** `actor_sub`
- **Request context field:** `requestId` (runtime), `request_id` (DB)

## Files updated

- `docs/architecture/system.md`
  - Added explicit naming conventions section.
  - Updated ownership check wording to `owner_id = actorSub`.
  - Fixed system diagram label to match current phased ops design (`Audit + optional idempotency`).

- `docs/architecture/modules/identity-access-module.md`
  - `AuthenticatedPrincipal` now uses `actorSub` consistently.
  - Kept `/me` response as `sub` (OIDC-standard), with explicit mapping note to `actorSub`.

- `docs/architecture/modules/document-module.md`
  - Renamed all service/repository method params from `ownerSub` to `actorSub`.
  - Updated class diagram to use `actorSub`.
  - Added explicit note: `actorSub` maps to `owner_id` in SQL predicates.

- `docs/architecture/modules/input-preferences-module.md`
  - Renamed all service/repository method params from `ownerSub` to `actorSub`.
  - Updated class diagram to use `actorSub`.
  - Added explicit mapping note to `owner_id`.

## Also retained previous fixes

- Index links in `docs/architecture/backend-architecture.md` remain corrected.
- `Operational Store Module` exists and is linked.
- API-edge persistence remains phased (no mandatory rate-counter persistence in V1).

If you want, I can do one final **style pass** to align method naming conventions too (for example `getByOwner` vs `getOwned` vs `findByOwner`) without changing semantics.

rkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"call_7f3u8xGN7ReqVPToOr2ylZBa\\\\nfc_044e06f34a8c5a850169ae203f345081978a3dacab6b5c4dab\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"call_7f3u8xGN7ReqVPToOr2ylZBa\\\\nfc_044e06f34a8c5a850169ae203f345081978a3dacab6b5c4dab\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"// IndexedDB utilities for GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport interface Document {\\\\\\\\n  id: string;\\\\\\\\n  title: string;\\\\\\\\n  content: string;\\\\\\\\n  language: 'en' | 'de' | 'ru';\\\\\\\\n  createdAt: number;\\\\\\\\n  updatedAt: number;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nconst DB_NAME = 'GlossaDocs';\\\\\\\\nconst STORE_NAME = 'documents';\\\\\\\\nconst DB_VERSION = 1;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nlet dbInstance: IDBDatabase | null = null;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Initialize the IndexedDB database\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function initDB(): Promise&lt;IDBDatabase&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  if (dbInstance) {\\\\\\\\n    return dbInstance;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onerror = () =&gt; reject(request.error);\\\\\\\\n    request.onsuccess = () =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      dbInstance = request.result;\\\\\\\\n      resolve(request.result);\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onupgradeneeded = (event) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      const db = (event.target as IDBOpenDBRequest).result;\\\\\\\\n      if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(STORE_NAME)) {\\\\\\\\n        const objectStore = db.createObjectStore(STORE_NAME, { keyPath: 'id' });\\\\\\\\n        objectStore.createIndex('updatedAt', 'updatedAt', { unique: false });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Helper function to execute an IndexedDB transaction\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nasync function executeTransaction&lt;T&gt;(\\\\\\\\n  mode: IDBTransactionMode,\\\\\\\\n  operation: (store: IDBObjectStore) =&gt; IDBRequest&lt;T&gt;\\\\\\\\n): Promise&lt;T&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const db = await initDB();\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const transaction = db.transaction([STORE_NAME], mode);\\\\\\\\n    const objectStore = transaction.objectStore(STORE_NAME);\\\\\\\\n    const request = operation(objectStore);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    request.onerror = () =&gt; reject(request.error);\\\\\\\\n    request.onsuccess = () =&gt; resolve(request.result);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Get all documents sorted by most recently updated\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function getAllDocuments(): Promise&lt;Document[]&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const docs = await executeTransaction&lt;Document[]&gt;('readonly', (store) =&gt; store.getAll());\\\\\\\\n  // Sort by most recently updated\\\\\\\\n  return docs.sort((a, b) =&gt; b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Get a single document by ID\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function getDocument(id: string): Promise&lt;Document | null&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  const result = await executeTransaction&lt;Document | undefined&gt;('readonly', (store) =&gt;\\\\\\\\n    store.get(id)\\\\\\\\n  );\\\\\\\\n  return result || null;\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Save or update a document\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function saveDocument(doc: Document): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  await executeTransaction&lt;IDBValidKey&gt;('readwrite', (store) =&gt; store.put(doc));\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Delete a document by ID\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport async function deleteDocument(id: string): Promise&lt;void&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  await executeTransaction&lt;undefined&gt;('readwrite', (store) =&gt; store.delete(id));\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Generate a unique ID for a document\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function generateId(): string {\\\\\\\\n  return Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * Import a document from a JSON file\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\nexport function importDocument(file: File): Promise&lt;Document&gt; {\\\\\\\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    const reader = new FileReader();\\\\\\\\n    reader.onload = (e) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n      try {\\\\\\\\n        const doc = JSON.parse(e.target?.result as string) as Document;\\\\\\\\n        // Generate new ID to avoid conflicts\\\\\\\\n        doc.id = generateId();\\\\\\\\n        doc.updatedAt = Date.now();\\\\\\\\n        resolve(doc);\\\\\\\\n      } catch (error) {\\\\\\\\n        reject(new Error('Invalid document format'));\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    reader.onerror = () =&gt; reject(reader.error);\\\\\\\\n    reader.readAsText(file);\\\\\\\\n  });\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":123}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/src/app/utils/db.ts\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"// GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is a browser-based document editor inspired by Google Docs, with a product focus on better multilingual writing workflows.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe current codebase is a working local-first UI prototype with rich text editing, document storage in the browser, and language-aware UX foundations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What The Project Is\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs aims to make multilingual writing feel native instead of bolted on. The core concept is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Let users choose language intent per document.\\\\\\\\n- Build toward integrated non-Latin input methods (starting with Russian in planned stories).\\\\\\\\n- Preserve a familiar docs-style experience (document list, editor, autosave, export/import).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Implementation (In This Repo)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Rich text editor built on &#96;contentEditable&#96; with formatting controls:\\\\\\\\n  - Bold, italic, underline\\\\\\\\n  - Font size, alignment, and line spacing\\\\\\\\n  - Image insertion (PNG/JPEG, with size cycling)\\\\\\\\n- Document management UI:\\\\\\\\n  - Create new document\\\\\\\\n  - Open existing document\\\\\\\\n  - Delete document\\\\\\\\n  - Edit document title\\\\\\\\n- Persistence:\\\\\\\\n  - Browser IndexedDB storage (&#96;GlossaDocs&#96; database)\\\\\\\\n  - Auto-save after 10 seconds of unsaved changes\\\\\\\\n  - Manual save button and saved/unsaved status indicators\\\\\\\\n- Import/Export:\\\\\\\\n  - Import &#96;.txt&#96; and &#96;.docx&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Export &#96;.txt&#96;, &#96;.docx&#96;, and &#96;.pdf&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Language support (current UI):\\\\\\\\n  - Per-document language selector for &#96;en&#96;, &#96;de&#96;, &#96;ru&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Direction handling scaffold exists for future RTL languages\\\\\\\\n- Auth state:\\\\\\\\n  - Login/guest flow is currently simulated with local storage placeholders (no real backend auth yet)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Tech Stack\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- React + TypeScript\\\\\\\\n- Vite\\\\\\\\n- IndexedDB for local document persistence\\\\\\\\n- UI libraries: MUI, Radix, Tailwind ecosystem utilities\\\\\\\\n- File conversion/export libraries:\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;mammoth&#96; for &#96;.docx&#96; import\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;docx&#96; and &#96;jspdf&#96; for export\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Running Locally\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm install&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Start the dev server:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm run dev&#96;\\\\\\\\n3. Open in browser:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;http://localhost:5173/&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNote: this repo is configured to run Vite with host exposure so local browser access works from &#96;npm run dev&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Limitations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- No backend or real authentication yet.\\\\\\\\n- No collaboration/sharing yet.\\\\\\\\n- Language features are in progress; current language selection is foundational, with advanced input-method behavior planned.\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":65}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"C:/Users/tommy/CS485/GlossaDocs/README.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# GlossaDocs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs is a browser-based document editor inspired by Google Docs, with a product focus on better multilingual writing workflows.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe current codebase is a working local-first UI prototype with rich text editing, document storage in the browser, and language-aware UX foundations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What The Project Is\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGlossaDocs aims to make multilingual writing feel native instead of bolted on. The core concept is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Let users choose language intent per document.\\\\\\\\n- Build toward integrated non-Latin input methods (starting with Russian in planned stories).\\\\\\\\n- Preserve a familiar docs-style experience (document list, editor, autosave, export/import).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Current Implementation (In This Repo)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Rich text editor built on &#96;contentEditable&#96; with formatting controls:\\\\\\\\n  - Bold, italic, underline\\\\\\\\n  - Font size, alignment, and line spacing\\\\\\\\n  - Image insertion (PNG/JPEG, with size cycling)\\\\\\\\n- Document management UI:\\\\\\\\n  - Create new document\\\\\\\\n  - Open existing document\\\\\\\\n  - Delete document\\\\\\\\n  - Edit document title\\\\\\\\n- Persistence:\\\\\\\\n  - Browser IndexedDB storage (&#96;GlossaDocs&#96; database)\\\\\\\\n  - Auto-save after 10 seconds of unsaved changes\\\\\\\\n  - Manual save button and saved/unsaved status indicators\\\\\\\\n- Import/Export:\\\\\\\\n  - Import &#96;.txt&#96; and &#96;.docx&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Export &#96;.txt&#96;, &#96;.docx&#96;, and &#96;.pdf&#96;\\\\\\\\n- Language support (current UI):\\\\\\\\n  - Per-document language selector for &#96;en&#96;, &#96;de&#96;, &#96;ru&#96;\\\\\\\\n  - Direction handling scaffold exists for future RTL languages\\\\\\\\n- Auth state:\\\\\\\\n  - Login/guest flow is currently simulated with local storage placeholders (no real backend auth yet)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Tech Stack\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- React + TypeScript\\\\\\\\n- Vite\\\\\\\\n- IndexedDB for local document persistence\\\\\\\\n- UI libraries: MUI, Radix, Tailwind ecosystem utilities\\\\\\\\n- File conversion/export libraries:\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;mammoth&#96; for &#96;.docx&#96; import\\\\\\\\n  - &#96;docx&#96; and &#96;jspdf&#96; for export\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Running Locally\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm install&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Start the dev server:\\\\\\\\n   - &#96;npm run dev&#96;\\\\\\\\n3.
